lockingPolicy.lockReason: Bugfix-fastlane real code fix; scenarios drive the cross-tenant isolation red→green proof.

tdd.redGreenEvidence: go test re-runs captured in report.md '## Test Evidence': RED = TestComputeDedupKey_VariesByOwner + TestDedupStore_CrossOwnerIsolation FAIL with owner absent from the preimage; GREEN = both PASS after owner_user_id is written first into the preimage.

scenarios.title: Dedup key varies by owner_user_id (cross-tenant divergence + same-owner determinism)
scenarios.liveTestExpectation: Two owners with identical (url, content_type, device, bucket) produce DIFFERENT keys; the same owner with identical inputs produces the SAME key.

scenarios.title: Store-level cross-tenant isolation: owner B gets its own artifact, same-owner repeat dedups
scenarios.liveTestExpectation: Owner A publishes (isDup=false, art-A); owner B same tuple publishes (isDup=false, art-B, NOT art-A); owner A repeat dedups (isDup=true, art-A).

scenarios.title: Handler fail-loud rejection of an empty owner (no fallback owner id)
scenarios.liveTestExpectation: A session with empty UserID passes the ServeHTTP 401 guard but the item is rejected with error 'owner_required' and never published.

scenarios.title: Live-Postgres cross-owner isolation: two owners resolve to two distinct rows + artifact_ids
scenarios.liveTestExpectation: Against live Postgres: owner A and owner B with an identical tuple produce two distinct raw_ingest_dedup rows and two distinct artifact_ids, neither publish skipped. Runs in CI (DATABASE_URL set); skips locally otherwise.

scenarios.title: Regression: single-owner-multi-device (Chrome Sync) keys still diverge
scenarios.liveTestExpectation: For one owner, two distinct devices still produce DIFFERENT keys — the legitimate same-owner-multi-device case is preserved (no regression).
